feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: America's lifespan has doubled since 1776 — experts reveal what changed
entry.summary: U.S. life expectancy has doubled since 1776 thanks to vaccines, antibiotics, sanitation and clean water that reduced infectious disease deaths.

entry.title: AI may spot deadly heart risk in a routine ECG
entry.summary: UC Berkeley researchers trained AI to detect hidden warning signs of sudden cardiac death in routine ECG tests, according to a new study published in Nature.

entry.title: Column | What a gastroenterologist wants you to know about bidets
entry.summary: I’ve received so many questions from people who are bidet-curious but not yet bidet-ready.

entry.title: 5 Labs Every Woman 40+ Should Be Getting & Why You Should Ask For Them
entry.summary: Your doctor means well, but you still need to advocate for yourself. Here's what to ask for.

entry.title: Scotland was the 'murder capital of Europe'. Then it started treating violence like a disease
entry.summary: In the early 2000s you were more three times more likely to be assaulted in Scotland than in the US, but the country now ranks among some of the safest in the world.

entry.title: People are taking allergy and heartburn pills for PMS. Could it work?
entry.summary: Can the mix really help with premenstrual woes? Theoretically, the link between histamine and hormones could explain why the trendy DIY treatment seems to help some women.

entry.title: Millions take omega-3 fish oil for brain health but a new study found no benefit
entry.summary: Fish oil supplements successfully delivered omega-3s to the brain, but a two-year study found no meaningful benefits for memory, cognition, or Alzheimer’s-related brain changes. The results challenge the idea that fish oil pills can help prevent Alzheimer’s a…

entry.title: Neuroscientists long believed you are born with all the neurons you will ever have — in 2025, researchers found newly formed neurons in the brains of adults as old as 78, overturning decades of settled thinking
entry.summary: For most of the twentieth century, the rule seemed simple. You are born with all the neurons you will ever have, and the adult brain makes no new ones. In July 2025, a team at the Karolinska Institutet in Sweden reported finding the cells that give rise to ne…

entry.title: Taking vitamin D and calcium every day? Doctors reveal why supplements may not protect bones the way many believe
entry.summary: For years, vitamin D and calcium tablets have enjoyed an almost magical reputation. A certain age arrives, knees begin to ache, or a health check-up mentions "weak bones," and the small white tablets often become part of the daily routine. Many people take th…

entry.title: KosAbility: Ticks and TBDs increasing while a new vaccine offers hope for those who will use it
entry.summary: The likelihood of encountering ticks and new reasons to avoid ticks are higher than ever this year and the news media is paying attention. In one week, I saw a variety of news stories warning us that…

entry.title: Some medications can make it harder for your body to handle extreme heat, experts say
entry.summary: The Brief

entry.title: The Cells In Your Body Fade With Age, But There May Be a Way to Reverse It
entry.summary: To properly understand aging, we have to understand what is slowly unravelling in individual cells.

entry.title: Injectable silk-kudzu hydrogel achieves complete wound closure in laboratory tests
entry.summary: Researchers at the Terasaki Institute for Biomedical Innovation have developed an injectable hydrogel, a water-based gel material, made from silk proteins and a plant-derived compound. In laboratory tests, the material promoted complete wound closure within 7…

entry.title: Lingering distrust, low vaccine rates threaten containment of Lancaster measles outbreak
entry.summary: False

entry.title: Going through menopause? Vital therapy slashes risk of low bone density by 69%
entry.summary: New research suggests a vital therapy for menopausal women could significantly improve bone density — a crucial health factor for aging women.

entry.title: Common pesticide linked to more than double the risk of Parkinson’s disease
entry.summary: Scientists at UCLA have linked long-term exposure to the pesticide chlorpyrifos with a sharply increased risk of Parkinson’s disease. People exposed to the chemical near their homes were more than twice as likely to develop the condition. Laboratory studies s…

entry.title: A Dietitian Shares 4 Creatine Benefits for Women as They Age
entry.summary: Learn how creatine may support healthy aging in women, including its effects on muscle, brain, bone, and mood—plus safety, risks, and how much to take.

entry.title: 62-Year-Old Chennai Man Proves You Don't Need A Gym To Stay Fit
entry.summary: The Chennai resident believes good health starts with moving your body every day

entry.title: The low-cal dessert that’s good for your gut health, eases joint pain — and can fight aging
entry.summary: One dessert stands above the rest, delivering a boost to gut health, joint pain relief and anti-aging benefits — and it’s probably already in your pantry.

entry.title: I Thought I Was Ahead of the Curve on Sun Protection. It Was Already Too Late.
entry.summary: A longtime angler thought decades of sunscreen made him careful. A basal cell carcinoma diagnosis on his jawline said otherwise — and changed how he fishes.

entry.title: 6 Ways Your Body Can Change After Starting a High-Protein Diet
entry.summary: Adding more protein to your diet can cause several changes in your body, such as better muscle repair and potential digestive issues.

entry.title: 6 Dietitian-Approved High-Protein Foods That Are Less Than $5 Per Serving
entry.summary: These dietitian-approved high-protein foods all cost less than $5 per serving and are easy to add to your everyday meals.

entry.title: Shining blue light on gold-graphene nanodots achieves wound healing trifecta
entry.summary: Closing wounds, burns and deep cuts isn't enough to kick-start healing. A wound needs a clean environment, free of bacterial infection and interruption. That calls for three components working together—one to kill bacteria, one to clean the wound and one to s…

entry.title: Dietitians Reveal the Bean With the Most Fiber—and How to Add It to Your Meals
entry.summary: Discover the type of bean that packs the most fiber, according to registered dietitians. This healthy bean is also a great source of iron and potassium.

entry.title: Could 'humanmaxxing' actually help you live longer? Here's what experts say
entry.summary: Humanmaxxing is the latest wellness trend focused on maximizing human potential through biohacking, longevity science and advanced medical treatments.

entry.title: There’s an Easy Way to Reduce Summer Party Anxiety for Everyone. And We Already Have the Technology.
entry.summary: Do this at your next gathering—I beg you.

entry.title: Here are the at-home medical items nurses swear by
entry.summary: These health professionals rely on Vicks VapoRub, Benadryl, aloe vera gel and many other products to treat common ailments.

entry.title: Oral GLP-1 pill delivers nearly 12 percent weight loss in 36 weeks, study finds
entry.summary: The study found that the drug stimulates insulin secretion, suppresses appetite and increases satisfaction to promote weight loss.

entry.title: Rise in cancer in younger adults may be explained by faster 'biological aging,' early study hints
entry.summary: Researchers report that younger adults with older‑than‑expected biological ages are more likely to develop early‑onset lung, gastrointestinal and uterine cancers, but more research is needed.

entry.title: What Drinking Oat Milk Every Day Does to Your Body, According to Nutrition Experts
entry.summary: If you drink oat milk every day, find out how it affects your heart health, blood sugar, digestion, and more, according to registered dietitians. Also, what to look for on the label to help you buy the best oat milk.

entry.title: Four easy ways to live longer
entry.summary: “Longevity” doesn’t need to be an elaborate science experiment.

entry.title: 10 Signs You're Not Getting Enough Magnesium—and What to Do About It
entry.summary: Magnesium is an essential mineral, but many of us are missing the mark. Discover the warning signs of not getting enough magnesium and how to fix it.

entry.title: This rare tick-borne disease is on the rise in the U.S.
entry.summary: Cases of Powassan virus have hit the news in recent months because of the potentially life-altering, sometimes fatal, symptoms.

entry.title: The Best Tea for Lowering High Blood Pressure, Recommended by a Dietitian
entry.summary: Sipping tea is one way to help manage blood pressure naturally. Find out why green tea tops our list as the best choice for your heart.

entry.title: Slow Breathing Can Rewire Your Brain and Change the Choices You Make
entry.summary: Extended exhalation increases reward sensitivity and heart rate variability, leading to bolder decision-making through measurable changes in brain activity. Researchers from the German Institute of Human Nutrition Potsdam-Rehbruecke (DIfE) and Charité – Unive…

entry.title: Can 30 seconds of exercise actually make a difference? Here’s what experts say
entry.summary: From intensity to timing, researchers share the simple strategies that can help you get more health benefits from short bursts of exercise.

entry.title: Here's What You Should Know If You Use Your Hands To Clean Your Body In The Shower
entry.summary: Doctors reveal the washing method thats REALLY best for your skin — and it might surprise you.

entry.title: Parasite That Affects Cognition Has Quietly Infected Billions of People
entry.summary: A parasite transmitted via cat turds has silently infected billions of people, and scientists are scrambling to contain it.

entry.title: Shingles vaccine may also offer protection against dementia, studies increasingly show
entry.summary: The shingles vaccine may significantly help protect older adults against dementia, a growing body of evidence shows

entry.title: Eggs score 10/10, quinoa just 4/10: Dietitian rates 9 popular protein sources and reveals which ones are worth eating | Health
entry.summary: From sprouted moong to Greek yoghurt, dietitian Shweta J Panchal rates nine protein-rich foods and reveals which ones deserve a place on your plate. | Health

entry.title: People with insecure relationship habits tend to have more children, study finds
entry.summary: Research across Japan, Canada, and the United States shows that fearful and preoccupied relationship traits actually predict larger family sizes. The findings suggest cultural norms heavily influence how our emotional habits impact reproductive success.

entry.title: Dad, 28, Makes Ultimate Birthday Sacrifice to Save His Baby Son’s Life: ‘Felt Like a Ticking Time Bomb’ (Exclusive)
entry.summary: “We went from crying tears of fear to crying tears of relief and joy,” Brian Carstens said after donating part of his liver to save his baby son

entry.title: Another B vitamin linked to worse outcomes for cancer
entry.summary: While B12 is prevalent in the blood of some cancer patients, researchers remain divided as to whether it causes or aids cancer growth.

entry.title: Brain-boosting foods: Hyderabad neurologist explains why coffee and dark chocolate make the list, why coc
entry.summary: Wellness News: Most brain health advice online sounds like a sales pitch. Eat this one superfood, take this one supplement, and your memory will thank you forever. S.

entry.title: Endocrinologists Share 5 Habits to Break for Healthier Blood Sugar
entry.summary: Wondering how to support healthy blood sugar? Endocrinologists explain five common habits to rethink and what to do instead.

entry.title: Suspected brain cancer turns out to be pork tapeworm infestation
entry.summary: Doctors in Spain suspected that a man suffering from chronic headaches had brain cancer. A scan showed a number of poorly-defined lesions, typical of cancer that has spread from elsewhere…

entry.title: After 7-year-old's fight with brain cancer, Cleveland family advocates for blood donations
entry.summary: For the almost 7-year-old Lincoln Rodgers and his Cleveland family, his battle with cancer began early on in life.In 2024, we first told you about Lincoln in a

entry.title: New vitamin B12 therapy shows promise against deadly brain cancer
entry.summary: Researchers have identified a vitamin B12–based compound that can cross the blood-brain barrier and home in on glioblastoma tumors. In animal studies, the compound accumulated preferentially in tumor tissue and delivered sustained nitric oxide directly to can…

entry.title: Arizona health officials investigate suspected pneumonic plague case
entry.summary: Yavapai County Community Health Services said Friday that a county resident is thought to have contracted the infection.

entry.title: Peptide alternative to antibiotics could combat antimicrobial resistance crisis
entry.summary: A University of Alberta research team has designed a promising alternative for treating antimicrobial-resistant infections, a pressing global health issue. In a paper recently published in Cell Biomaterials, the team describes preclinical testing results for …
